bugs.name: Explore section empty

history.root_cause: Search prompts for explore pool were too narrow, returning no results or all results lacked URLs
history.fix_applied: Broadened explore search prompt to include more outlets and relaxed freshness window; added explore URL enforcement in validate_brief()

fix_recipe: Check search_news() explore prompt — has the source list in config.json explore_pool been modified or shrunk?
Check if Claude web search is returning empty for explore queries — look at raw_results['explore'] in stdout logs
Check if validate_brief() is removing ALL explore stories via NOURL filter — look for NOURL removals in stdout
Try: widen the explore search date range or add more outlets to explore_pool in config.json

bugs.name: Paywalled content in explore
bugs.description: Explore URLs pointing to known paywall domains that readers cannot access without subscription

history.root_cause: Explore pool includes outlets with hard paywalls; no domain-level filtering existed
history.fix_applied: Added domain-level filtering for known paywall sites in explore URL validation

fix_recipe: Check validate_brief() explore URL enforcement — is the paywall filter still present?
Check if new paywalled domains have been added to config.json explore_pool without updating the filter
Add the offending domain to flagged_domains in this known_bugs.json entry
If the domain is a subscriber source (Nisha has access), consider adding it to an allowlist instead

bugs.name: NBA score display formatting
bugs.description: NBA scores present in data but HTML rendering is broken or unreadable in email

history.root_cause: Inline styles in generate_email_html() produce poor layout in email clients

fix_recipe: Review NBA score rendering in generate_email_html() — search for 'nba_scores' in the function
Test generated HTML in email preview tools (Litmus, Email on Acid, or send a test brief)
Consider switching to a table-based layout for better email client compatibility
Check that away_abbr, home_abbr, away_score, home_score fields are all present and non-null

bugs.name: Cross-section bleed
bugs.description: Story placed in a section that doesn't match its source_section tag from search

history.root_cause: Claude synthesis ignored section boundaries and moved stories between sections based on perceived relevance
history.fix_applied: Added source_section tagging in search_news() and strict SECTION ASSIGNMENT RULE in synthesis prompt; validate_brief() dedup uses source_section for tiebreaking

fix_recipe: Check search_news() — is source_section still being tagged on each story result?
Check synthesise_brief() prompt — is the SECTION ASSIGNMENT RULE still present and strongly worded?
Check validate_brief() dedup logic — is it correctly using source_section for tiebreaking when removing duplicates?
If Claude is ignoring the prompt rule: strengthen wording or add a post-synthesis re-sort that forces stories back to their tagged section

bugs.name: Same story in multiple sections
bugs.description: The same story appears in two different sections with different headlines but covering the same event, even when source_section tags are correct (searched separately, both legitimate)

history.root_cause: The same event gets searched in multiple categories (e.g. world and india) and Claude returns it in both with different framing. validate_brief() dedup catches keyword overlap but misses semantically identical stories with different wording.
history.fix_applied: Added Jaccard similarity check (>0.40 threshold) in validate_brief() cross-section dedup; source_section tiebreaking removes the copy from the less natural section

fix_recipe: Check validate_brief() cross-section dedup — is the Jaccard threshold still 0.40? It may be too high for stories with different framing of the same event
Look at today's brief for duplicate stories across sections — if the headlines share few keywords but describe the same event, the keyword-based approach is insufficient
Consider lowering the Jaccard threshold to 0.30, or adding entity-based dedup (matching on key names, places, numbers)
Check if synthesis prompt has a rule against repeating the same event across sections — strengthen it if missing

bugs.name: Vague or hallucinated sources
bugs.description: Stories with no verifiable URL and only generic 'Additional:' source attribution slip through validation

detection.check: any(_has_vague_source(s) for sec in brief_data.get('sections', []) for s in sec.get('stories', []))

history.root_cause: Claude fabricated stories or cited generic sources when section coverage was thin and the prompt encouraged filling all slots
history.fix_applied: Added _has_vague_source() filter in validate_brief() step 3; synthesis prompt updated with PLACEHOLDER RULE banning filler stories

fix_recipe: Check validate_brief() step 3 — is the vague-source filter still running? Look for the _has_vague_source() call
Check if _has_vague_source() logic has been accidentally loosened (e.g. threshold changed)
Check synthesis prompt PLACEHOLDER RULE — if it was weakened, Claude may be fabricating to fill thin sections
If stories are getting through with URLs but the URLs are fake: would need a URL reachability check (costly, consider as last resort)

bugs.name: Empty news section after validation
bugs.description: One or more main news sections (world, india, tech, business, science, sports) have 0 stories after validation — the section renders as an empty header in the email

detection.details_expr: 'Empty sections: ' + ', '.join(sec['id'] for sec in brief_data.get('sections', []) if len(sec.get('stories', [])) == 0)

history.root_cause: Claude synthesis returns stories without source URLs; validate_brief() VAGUE filter correctly removes them, but when ALL stories in a section lack URLs the entire section ends up empty. Search succeeds, synthesis succeeds, but URL population is inconsistent.

fix_recipe: Check validate_brief() VAGUE removal log in workflow stdout — count how many stories per section were removed

If most removals are VAGUE: the synthesis prompt is not populating sources[].url fields. Strengthen the URL requirement in the synthesis prompt or add a fallback that constructs URLs from outlet + headline
If most removals are DEDUP/META: the section had too few unique stories to begin with. Increase the story count requested per section in the search prompt
Consider a fallback: if a section drops to 0 stories post-validation, re-run search for that section with a broader query before giving up
Nuclear option: loosen _has_vague_source() to allow stories with a named (non-Additional) source even without a URL — trades verifiability for coverage
